metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command to AC1 (e.g. `cd <repo-root> && rebar verify-identity; echo "exit: $?"` with assertion `exit code != 2`) so the runtime behavior claim is verifiably evidenced, not just documented.
findings.finding: AC1 asserts 'rebar verify-identity scans real events rather than exiting 2 (store-not-found)' as a completion-relevant claim, but neither the work bullets nor the AC name a concrete proving command that would produce evidence of this (e.g., run the command, capture exit code, compare to 2). The AC is partially verifiable (the doc note), but the runtime behavior claim is unanchored.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC1: 'Confirmed (and noted in docs/identity.md) that … rebar verify-identity scans real events rather than exiting 2 (store-not-found).'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC1 to include: 'Verified by running `rebar verify-identity` in CI or locally with the worktree mounted and confirming exit code is 0 (not 2); paste the exit-code line from the run into the PR description or docs/identity.md.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] When adding the verify-identity CI gate section to `docs/identity.md`, either update the existing 'Opt-in enforcement' section to use `rebar verify-identity` as the canonical name (with `verify-authorship` noted as a back-compat alias), or add a cross-reference note so the two sections are consistent.
findings.finding: The existing `docs/identity.md` 'Opt-in enforcement' section (lines 127–145) refers to the command as `rebar verify-authorship`, but the plan's new CI-gate section will use `rebar verify-identity`. The plan does not acknowledge this naming inconsistency within the doc target, leaving the document with two different command names for the same gate in adjacent sections.
findings.location: docs/identity.md lines 127–145; plan Work section / AC lines
findings.suggested_fix: In the existing 'Opt-in enforcement' section, change '`rebar verify-authorship`' to '`rebar verify-identity`' (or '`rebar verify-identity` (alias: `verify-authorship`)') so the canonical name is consistent with the new CI gate section the plan adds.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align the ticket title with its full scope — e.g. 'Confirm and document verify-identity CI gate (store mount, posture, grandfathering)' — so it matches the three ACs.
findings.finding: The plan title ('Confirm verify-identity CI gate mounts the tickets store') understates the plan's actual scope: the body and ACs also cover documenting the current gate posture (advisory vs. enforcing) and the grandfathering-boundary mechanism — work that goes materially beyond 'mounts the tickets store.'
findings.location: Plan title vs. Work section and ACs 2–3
findings.suggested_fix: Rename the ticket title to: 'Confirm and document verify-identity CI gate: store mount, gate posture, and grandfathering boundary'

findings.finding: The plan asserts the workflow 'hard-forces `--require-authenticated` regardless of config', but the current verify-identity.yaml (lines 54-56) explicitly does the OPPOSITE: it passes NO --require-authenticated flag and reads enforcement posture from rebar.toml (`identity.require_authenticated`). This is a directly contradicted codebase assertion.
findings.location: Plan body paragraph 1 ('hard-forces `--require-authenticated` regardless of config') vs .github/workflows/verify-identity.yaml lines 54-56
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'hard-forces `--require-authenticated` regardless of config' with 'reads enforcement posture from `identity.require_authenticated` in rebar.toml (currently false — advisory mode)' and update AC2 to ask whether the gate posture in rebar.toml matches the intended rollout state.

findings.finding: The plan asserts that '`ENFORCE_SINCE` grandfathering is driven by the GitHub Actions repo variable `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE`', but no such variable reference exists anywhere in the repo — not in verify-identity.yaml, not in any other workflow. The workflow passes bare `rebar verify-identity` with no `--since` flag; the implementation reads `identity.enforce_since` from rebar.toml (rebar.toml line 97, verify_authorship.py line 465). The `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` claim is entirely ungrounded.
findings.location: Plan body bullet 3 ('`vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` (CI) vs `identity.enforce_since` (local)') and AC3
findings.suggested_fix: If the intent is to allow per-environment override without changing rebar.toml, rewrite AC3 as: 'Decide whether a `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` GitHub Actions variable override is needed; if yes, add it to the workflow and document the CI-vs-local split; if no, document that both CI and local read `identity.enforce_since` from rebar.toml.'

findings.finding: The plan's opening premise states the workflow 'hard-forces `--require-authenticated` regardless of config', but `verify-identity.yaml` line 54–56 explicitly documents the OPPOSITE: no `--require-authenticated` flag is passed; enforcement follows `rebar.toml`. This premise is factually wrong and causes the plan to misdescribe the current gate state (AC2 scope).
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'hard-forces `--require-authenticated` regardless of config' with 'reads enforcement posture from `identity.require_authenticated` in rebar.toml (currently false / advisory)'.

findings.finding: `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` (a GitHub Actions repository variable) is referenced in the plan body and all three ACs, but no such variable exists anywhere in the codebase. The actual CI-side override mechanism is the environment variable `REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE`, not a GitHub Actions `vars.*` context value. This is a hallucinated edit target.
findings.location: Plan body bullet 3 / AC2 / AC3 / rebar.toml line 96 / src/rebar/_config_schema.py line 321
findings.suggested_fix: In AC2 and AC3, s/`vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` (repo variable)/`REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE` (environment variable)/. The CI distinction to document is: `REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE` env var (set in the workflow's `env:` block or as a repo secret/var) vs the `identity.enforce_since` config key in rebar.toml.

findings.finding: The plan body and AC3 assert that the `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` GitHub Actions repo variable drives the CI grandfathering boundary and instructs the implementer to confirm and document this — but the actual workflow contains no `${{ vars.ENFORCE_SINCE }}` expansion, no `--since` flag, and no env injection of that variable. The boundary is sourced exclusively from `rebar.toml`'s `identity.enforce_since` (or env override `REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE`). The mechanism described does not exist in the codebase.
findings.location: Plan body ('Confirm `ENFORCE_SINCE` grandfathering is driven by the GitHub Actions repo variable `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE`') and AC3 ('The `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` (CI) vs `identity.enforce_since` (local) distinction is documented')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the third plan bullet and AC3 with: 'Confirm and document that `identity.enforce_since` in `rebar.toml` governs the grandfathering boundary in both CI and locally, and that the env override `REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE` is the per-run escape hatch in both contexts — there is no separate `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` GitHub Actions variable.'

findings.finding: The plan asserts, without verification, that CI grandfathering is driven by a GitHub Actions repo variable `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE`. No such variable exists anywhere in the codebase: the actual CI override for the grandfather boundary is the env var `REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE`, already documented in `rebar.toml` and `_config_schema.py`, with `verify-identity.yaml` explicitly reading enforcement posture from `rebar.toml` and injecting no `vars.*` values.
findings.location: Plan body bullet: 'Confirm `ENFORCE_SINCE` grandfathering is driven by the GitHub Actions repo variable `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` (not `identity.enforce_since` in config) and document that.' / AC3: 'The `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` (CI) vs `identity.enforce_since` (local) distinction is documented.'
findings.suggested_fix: Revise the third scope bullet and AC3 to read: 'Confirm `ENFORCE_SINCE` grandfathering is driven by `REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE` (env var, settable in CI via `env:` block) rather than a separate GitHub Actions repo variable, and document that the local equivalent is `identity.enforce_since` in `rebar.toml`.' No new `vars.*` variable should be introduced.

findings.finding: The plan references `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` as a GitHub Actions repo variable that drives the CI grandfathering boundary, and AC3 requires documenting the 'vars.ENFORCE_SINCE (CI) vs identity.enforce_since (local) distinction' — but this variable does not exist anywhere in the codebase. The actual CI override mechanism is the environment variable `REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE` (confirmed in rebar.toml:96 and _config_schema.py:321). The plan invents a named decision-rule/protocol whose value vocabulary and wiring are never defined because the mechanism itself is phantom.
findings.location: Plan body bullet 3 and AC3: 'Confirm `ENFORCE_SINCE` grandfathering is driven by the GitHub Actions repo variable `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE`'
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC3 as: 'The `REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE` env var (CI) vs `identity.enforce_since` in rebar.toml (local) distinction is documented.' Update the plan body bullet to read: 'Confirm `ENFORCE_SINCE` grandfathering can be overridden in CI via the `REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE` environment variable (not a `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` Actions variable, which does not exist) and document that.'

findings.finding: The plan's opening premise states the workflow 'hard-forces `--require-authenticated` regardless of config,' presenting this as an established fact that needs confirmation. But `verify-identity.yaml` lines 53-57 explicitly do the opposite: no `--require-authenticated` flag is passed, and a co-located comment states 'enforcement posture … read from rebar.toml'. This false premise about the gate's decision-rule will cause AC2 ('Confirm current gate state') to be evaluated against a wrong baseline, potentially masking that the gate is currently advisory (exit 0) not enforcing.
findings.location: Plan body paragraph 1: 'hard-forces `--require-authenticated` regardless of config'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'hard-forces `--require-authenticated` regardless of config' with 'delegates enforcement to `identity.require_authenticated` in rebar.toml (currently false → advisory/exit-0)' and ensure AC2 explicitly states the gate is currently green/advisory.

findings.finding: The plan invents 'vars.ENFORCE_SINCE' as a GitHub Actions repo variable that does not exist in the codebase; the existing override mechanism is the env var REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE, already documented in rebar.toml and _config_schema.py. Documenting and potentially introducing vars.ENFORCE_SINCE would proliferate a redundant config surface.
findings.location: AC line: 'The `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` (CI) vs `identity.enforce_since` (local) distinction is documented.' / Plan body: 'Confirm `ENFORCE_SINCE` grandfathering is driven by the GitHub Actions repo variable `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE`'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'vars.ENFORCE_SINCE (CI)' with 'REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE env var (CI)' throughout the plan and AC3. The distinction to document is already: `identity.enforce_since` in rebar.toml (persisted project default) vs `REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE` env var (per-run CI override, e.g. set in the workflow's `env:` block).

findings.finding: The leaf plan states the workflow 'hard-forces `--require-authenticated` regardless of config,' which directly contradicts the parent's scope ('`identity.require_authenticated` STAYS False') and parent AC4 ('`require_authenticated` is NOT flipped in `rebar.toml`; schema default remains False'). The parent is authoritative; the leaf must not describe CI as hard-forcing a flag the parent explicitly forbids enabling.
findings.location: Leaf plan description — 'hard-forces `--require-authenticated` regardless of config'; also Leaf AC2 premise 'given enforcement is already hard-forced in CI'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'hard-forces `--require-authenticated` regardless of config' with 'reads enforcement posture from rebar.toml (`identity.require_authenticated`), which stays False per the parent story scope.' Update AC2 to remove the 'given enforcement is already hard-forced' premise.

findings.finding: The leaf introduces 'vars.ENFORCE_SINCE' as a GitHub Actions repo variable that overrides `identity.enforce_since` in CI (AC2: 'the `ENFORCE_SINCE` repo variable is set'; AC3: 'The `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` (CI) vs `identity.enforce_since` (local) distinction is documented'). This mechanism does not appear anywhere in the parent's plan. The existing override mechanism is the env var `REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE` (documented in `rebar.toml:96` and `_config_schema.py:321`). The leaf delivers and documents something the parent's plan does not contain.
findings.location: Leaf AC2 and AC3; leaf scope bullet 3 ('Confirm `ENFORCE_SINCE` grandfathering is driven by the GitHub Actions repo variable `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE`')
findings.suggested_fix: In AC2, change 'the `ENFORCE_SINCE` repo variable is set' to 'the `REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE` env var (or `identity.enforce_since` in rebar.toml) is set to an appropriate boundary.' In AC3, change 'The `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` (CI) vs `identity.enforce_since` (local) distinction is documented' to 'The `REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE` env var override of `identity.enforce_since` is documented in the workflow comment.'

findings.finding: The plan asserts that `ENFORCE_SINCE` grandfathering in CI 'is driven by the GitHub Actions repo variable `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE`', but no such repo variable exists or is wired anywhere in the codebase. The actual override mechanism is the env var `REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE` (documented in `rebar.toml:96` and `src/rebar/_config_schema.py:321`). The `verify-identity.yaml` workflow has no `env:` block mapping any `vars.*` value to that env var. Documenting or confirming a non-existent variable is a contradicted capability claim.
findings.location: Plan body: 'Confirm `ENFORCE_SINCE` grandfathering is driven by the GitHub Actions repo variable `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE`' / AC line: 'The `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` (CI) vs `identity.enforce_since` (local) distinction is documented.'
findings.suggested_fix: In the plan body and AC3, replace 'vars.ENFORCE_SINCE (CI)' with 'REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE env var (CI)' to match the actual documented override path. If a GitHub Actions repo variable is still desired, add an `env:` block to `verify-identity.yaml`: `REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE: ${{ vars.ENFORCE_SINCE }}`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'and note in docs/identity.md' to Work bullet 1 so the documentation deliverable for the mount-step confirmation is explicitly described (matching what AC1 already requires).
findings.finding: AC1 requires the mount-step confirmation to be 'noted in docs/identity.md', but the corresponding Work bullet (item 1) says only 'Confirm the mount step scans the store (a verify-identity run does not exit 2)' — it does not mention documenting this confirmation in docs/identity.md. The documentation deliverable implied by AC1 is not described in the Work section, creating a minor orphan criterion.
findings.location: Work section, bullet 1 vs Acceptance Criteria AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Change Work bullet 1 to: 'Confirm the mount step scans the store (a `verify-identity` run does not exit 2) and note this confirmation in `docs/identity.md` (verify-identity gate section).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit step to search `docs/identity.md` (and any other docs referencing the CI gate) for stale `vars.ENFORCE_SINCE` references and remove or correct them before closing the task.
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges that 'vars.ENFORCE_SINCE' was an incorrect reference used previously, but does not explicitly direct removal or correction of any stale occurrences of 'vars.ENFORCE_SINCE' that may already exist in docs/identity.md or other documentation files — only new correct content is committed to.
findings.location: Work section / AC set — no explicit 'remove stale references' step
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Work section: 'Search docs/ for any existing reference to vars.ENFORCE_SINCE and replace with the correct REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE env-var mechanism.' Add a corresponding AC: 'No documentation file contains the now-incorrect vars.ENFORCE_SINCE reference.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale to the plan (and have it reflected in docs/identity.md) for why enforcement posture is config-driven rather than hard-forced via CI flags — e.g. rebar.toml is the single source of project policy, the workflow file need not change when enforcement is flipped on, and the advisory→blocking rollout path is fully controlled by a one-line config edit.
findings.finding: The plan documents the config-driven CI posture (advisory because identity.require_authenticated=false, boundary via REBAR_IDENTITY_ENFORCE_SINCE) but never states a positive rationale for WHY this design is preferable to hard-forcing flags in the workflow. The AC asks docs/identity.md to document the posture; an implementer writing that section without understanding the design intent may produce a 'what' description rather than a 'why' one, reducing the documentation's operator value.
findings.location: Plan body — 'Enforcement is CONFIG-DRIVEN, not hard-forced' / AC line 2 (docs/identity.md documents current CI gate posture)

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
